name: Sao Paulo (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the night, recalling the bandeirantes who marched by night to surprise their enemies
the day, peace, and the colour shared with the bandeirantes' standard
appearing on the central canton and coat of arms
the wealth of the city, in the central canton's cross and arms ornamentation

description: Thirteen horizontal stripes alternating black and white, charged at the centre with a white square bearing a red cross and the coat of arms of the city of Sao Paulo.
history: Designed by the modernist artist and writer Guilherme de Almeida and adopted as the flag of the City of Sao Paulo on 8 July 1936, replacing earlier versions. The black-and-white stripes are taken from the historic flag of the bandeirantes; the same colours later inspired the flag of the State of Sao Paulo.

features.symbolism: Black and white were the colours of the flag carried by the bandeirantes, the 17th-century Sao Paulo expeditions that explored and expanded the interior of Brazil.

features.role: central white square charged with a red cross and the city's coat of arms
features.symbolism: The white square at the centre bears a red Cross of the Order of Christ and the municipal coat of arms, and is surrounded by four quarter-arcs evoking the Portuguese coat of arms.

features.role: Coat of arms of the City of Sao Paulo
features.symbolism: Bears a red shield charged with a gold escutcheon, surmounted by a mural crown; commemorates the city's foundation in 1554 by Jesuit missionaries.
